description:
You are acting as a junior database-administrator who has been asked to automate a very small part of the query-tuning workflow.

    1. A text file already exists at  
       /home/user/db_queries.log  

       Every line in this file has exactly three, comma-separated fields in the order  
       <query_id>,<duration_ms>,<query_string>

       Example line (spacing exactly as shown – there are no surrounding spaces except the single one after each comma):  
       42,2500,SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'PENDING';

    2. Management wants the three slowest (highest duration_ms) queries extracted into a separate plain-text report.  
       Your automation task is to write a **shell script** named  
       /home/user/optimize_slow_queries.sh

       The script must perform these actions, in order:

         • Read /home/user/db_queries.log  
         • Sort the lines by the second column (duration_ms) in *descending* numerical order  
         • Select only the first three lines of that sorted list  
         • Write those three lines verbatim to an output file called  
           /home/user/slow_queries_report.txt

       The script should contain no more than four simple shell commands (shebang and comments do not count toward that limit).

    3. After your script is created, run it once so that /home/user/slow_queries_report.txt is actually produced.

    4. Verification:  
       • slow_queries_report.txt must exist.  
       • It must contain exactly three lines, each identical to the corresponding line in the source log.  
       • The lines must appear from slowest to fastest (highest to lowest duration_ms).  
       • A trailing newline after the last line is mandatory.

       The automated tests will perform:  
         wc -l /home/user/slow_queries_report.txt  (should output “3”)  
         diff -q <(sort -t, -k2,2nr /home/user/db_queries.log | head -n 3) /home/user/slow_queries_report.txt (should output nothing)

    5. Deliverables to create:  
       /home/user/optimize_slow_queries.sh   – the executable script (max 4 commands)  
       /home/user/slow_queries_report.txt    – produced by running the script once

    Do not hard-code query IDs or durations; the script must work with the data actually present in the log file.  Remember to make the script executable.
name: task_000000_c9fb048e
